nodes.name: Time Complexity
nodes.definition:
It is a type of analysis to determine the approximate time required by an
algorithm to complete a given task when the number of inputs increases.
nodes.last_question_asked: If an algorithm performs a task in O(n) time, how would the execution time change if the input size were quadrupled, and what does this relationship suggest about the algorithm's efficiency compared to one that operates in O(log n) time?

nodes.name: Big O notation
nodes.definition:
It describes worst case scenario of an algorithm to complete a task when
input size increases.
nodes.last_question_asked: If you were tasked with selecting an algorithm for a system that processes real-time data, why might choosing an algorithm based solely on its Big O worst-case complexity lead to inefficient performance in practice, and what other factors might you need to consider?
